About Me

Cybersecurity Engineering student with a foundation in Python-based security automation, vulnerability testing, and Linux/Windows Hardening. Experienced in packet analysis, web/API testing, and supporting secure infrastructure via research, bug bounties, and CTF development.

I’m passionate about robotics security, digital forensics, and building secure systems. Currently researching ROS2 SLAM vulnerabilities and participating in bug bounty programs.

Education

George Mason University - Fairfax, VA
B.S. in Cybersecurity Engineering (Expected May 2026)

  • Dean’s List (Senior)
  • GPA: [From Transcript]
  • Relevant Coursework: Cryptography Fundamentals, Secure Software Engineering, Industrial Control Systems Security, System Security & Resilience, Data Breach Preparation & Communication, Secure RF Communications, Computer Networking, Operating Systems and Lab

Publications:

  • LiDAR Spoofing for Compromising SLAM on ROS2 Using Statistical Beam Adjustment and Temporal Modeling at the IEEE Symposium on Hardware-Oriented Security and Trust (2026)

Experience

GMU Robotics Research Assistant (August 2025 – Present)

  • Published research at the 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Hardware Oriented Security and Trust.
  • Performed security research on ROS2 SLAM systems by analyzing data flows and system architecture.
  • Built attacks to validate resilience against manipulated sensor data and unexpected network behavior.

Bugcrowd – Vulnerability Researcher (June 2025 – Aug 2025)

  • Identified and tested web and API vulnerabilities using Burp Suite and ZAP.
  • Conducted end-to-end analysis of HTTP traffic, session handling, and API requests.
  • Participated in responsible vulnerability logging and reporting processes.

Programming & CTF Competitor (2016 – Present)

  • Developed Python scripts for solving traffic analysis and log parsing challenges.
  • Performed OS hardening on Windows and Linux environments for enterprise-level security.
  • Created and published CTF challenges for PatriotCTF 2025 and deployed them on containers.

VEX Robotics Competition – Head Referee & Mentor (2022 – Present)

  • Supported competitions by assisting with wireless connectivity, networking, and system reliability.
  • Led volunteer teams, trained new referees, and managed competition infrastructure.
  • Mentored robotics teams, providing guidance on technical problem-solving and system reliability.

Skills

  • Programming & Scripting: Python, Java, C, Shell Scripting
  • Security Tools: Wireshark, Burp Suite, OWASP ZAP, Splunk, Ghidra
  • Automation and DevOps: Security Scripting, Container environments, CI/CD pipelines, GitHub, Containerized Environments
  • Networking & Systems: Computer networking, Unix/Linux, Windows administration
  • Certifications: CompTIA Security+, Google Cybersecurity Professional

Projects

  • Industrial Firewall Configuration: Built and configured a virtual industrial firewall to monitor traffic and enforce packet security between two VMs.
  • Local Server for Robotics Competitions: Developed and configured a secure locally hosted server solution to enable wireless communication across tournament manager devices.
  • CTF Container Configuration: Created several web-based CTF challenges designed to be hosted on a cloud solution using containers.

Current Interests

  • Robotics and ROS 2 security
  • Digital forensics
  • Network traffic analysis
  • Secure system configuration
  • Dockerized testing environments
  • Cybersecurity policy and law
